acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Canonical JSON helper emits stable bytes across runs.
- [ ] manifest_hash and content_hash computed per plan.
- [ ] Tests cover a minimal checkpoint meta example.
description:
**Problem**
Phase 6: implement canonical JSON helper and checkpoint manifest and meta structs. There is no checkpoint lane module yet, so manifest and meta definitions do not exist.

**Context**
- REALTIME_PLAN.md §13.3-13.5 (canonical JSON, manifest.json, meta.json)
**Files:** src/git/checkpoint/json_canon.rs (new), src/git/checkpoint/manifest.rs (new), src/git/checkpoint/meta.rs (new), src/git/checkpoint/mod.rs (new)
design:
**Design**
- Add `to_canon_json_bytes<T: Serialize>(v: &T) -> Vec<u8>` with sorted key ordering and no extra whitespace.
- Define Manifest and Meta structs with fields from plan; implement content_hash and manifest_hash calculation.
- Add tests verifying canonical JSON stability and hash determinism.
title: Phase 6: Checkpoint canonical JSON + manifest/meta structs

acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] git sync compiles without remap
- [ ] collisions handled by join

**Invariants to re-verify**
- [ ] Git sync no longer remaps IDs; collisions handled by join
description:
**Problem**
Git collision remap is incompatible with deterministic collision resolution.

**Files**
- src/git/collision.rs
- src/git/sync.rs
design: Remove remap usage and rely on CanonicalState::join + lineage tombstones for collisions.
title: Remove git collision remap path

closed_reason: Captured samply-test-suite.json; top samples in nanosleep/semwait + bd process wait time; identified IPC ping + extra create round-trip + test data-dir lock as bottlenecks.
description: Capture samply profile for cargo test and summarize hotpaths.
title: Profile test suite with samply
Fix failing test mutation_span_includes_realtime_context missing store_id

description:
**Problem**
Metrics are emitted as `tracing::info!` events and are filtered out unless global log level enables INFO. There is no configuration to enable/disable or tune metric emission.

**Design**
- Introduce `metrics` config (e.g., `metrics.enabled`, `metrics.level`, optional `metrics.target`).
- Add env overrides (e.g., `BD_METRICS`, `BD_METRICS_LEVEL`).
- Update `daemon::metrics` to respect config (no‑op when disabled, emit at configured level/target).

**Acceptance**
- [ ] Metrics emission can be toggled and leveled via config/env.
- [ ] Default behavior matches current output for daemon with INFO logging.
- [ ] Tests cover config/env overrides and disabled mode.

**Files:** src/config/schema.rs, src/config/merge.rs, src/telemetry.rs (if needed), src/daemon/metrics.rs
title: Observability: make metrics emission configurable

acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] A checked-in migration fixture contains a deleted legacy dep row sourced from a real historical store capture.
- [ ] The corpus README records the source repo/ref/OID or archive provenance for that fixture.
- [ ] The tombstone/deleted-dep migration test uses the sourced fixture instead of a purely contract-derived row.
description:
**Problem**
The migration fixture corpus now covers deleted legacy dep parsing with `tombstone_deleted_dep`, but that fixture is still contract-derived rather than sourced from an independently captured historical store. On 2026-03-11, a scan across current repos under `/Users/darin/Projects` and `/Users/darin/src/personal` found no current `refs/heads/beads/store` whose `deps.jsonl` still contained legacy deleted rows (`deleted_at` / `"deleted"`).

That means the current fixture is still validating the implemented parser contract, not a known historical store capture.

**Files**: crates/beads-rs/tests/integration/fixtures/legacy_store_corpus/README.md, crates/beads-rs/tests/integration/fixtures/legacy_store_corpus/tombstone_deleted_dep/*
design:
**Design**
Find or recover an independently captured historical legacy store that includes deleted dependency rows, reduce it into a checked-in fixture, and record the exact source repo/ref/OID in the corpus README.

If no such live repo remains, recover one from archived clones, exported store commits, or historical refs rather than continuing to rely on a synthetic row.
title: Migration fixture follow-up: source a real legacy deleted-dep capture
